title: Leucheria tenerifolia
taxonomy: Leucheria tenerifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae and is native to the Canary Islands. The genus Leucheria is closely related to Gnaphalium, but is often separated due to differences in leaf shape and flower structure. Leucheria tenerifolia is a relatively rare and endemic species found in specific regions of the Canary Islands. Its classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and distinct features within the Asteraceae family.
